content.text:
| MCP:xX9Bps | [{"number":2,"title":"database: add flush_all_caches() helper for dev environments","body":"Adds a `Walrus.flush_all_caches()` method that clears all cached
values across all walrus-managed Cache instances on the same
Redis connection.

Use case: during local development, when you've changed the body
of several cached functions and want to clear stale entries in
one call instead of busting them individually.

Closes #1 (cache stale on function body change).

## On using FLUSHDB

Yes, the implementation is one line: `self.flushdb()`. I considered
walking walrus's known cache prefixes and selectively `DEL`-ing them
but decided against it for three reasons:

1. **Walrus doesn't own the database**. Anyone using walrus already
   knows their Redis instance hosts a mix of walrus + non-walrus
   data; we don't have a registry of "which prefixes are walrus
   caches" because the prefix is `cache.cached(...)`-caller-defined.
   A "selective" implementation would just be a partial-FLUSHDB that
   misses edge cases.

2. **Dev-only convenience**. The method name signals that this is
   a local-dev helper. Anyone calling `flush_all_caches()` in prod
   has already taken responsibility for that — same as anyone
   calling `db.flushdb()` directly. We can't (and shouldn't) wrap
   every potentially-destructive method behind dev/prod env
   detection.

3. **Matches walrus convention**. Walrus inherits a lot of
   destructive-by-default behavior from `redis.Redis` (which is
   what `Database` extends). `flushdb`, `keys`, `scripts_flush`,
   `client_kill` are all available on `db` without guardrails. This
   PR's helper is in the same spirit.

Open to renaming if reviewers prefer (e.g.,
`Walrus.dev_flush_database()`) but the underlying operation should
stay as `flushdb`.

Tested locally on a fresh Redis instance; resets all keys as expected.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T12_R02_C02_A01/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/flush-all-caches","sha":"6880fc8494b5965f2f5d9d7fb26204a2ce5f09f3","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02_C02_A01"}},"base":{"ref":"master","sha":"d4337d11457995f1888b13e9f24b9429a1b195a6","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02_C02_A01"}},"created_at":"2026-05-22T11:17:22Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T11:17:22Z"}]

| MCP:xX9Bps | diff --git a/walrus/database.py b/walrus/database.py
| MCP:xX9Bps | index 04861bd..c67ecda 100644
| MCP:xX9Bps | --- a/walrus/database.py
| MCP:xX9Bps | +++ b/walrus/database.py
| MCP:xX9Bps | @@ -440,6 +440,15 @@ def stream_log(self, callback, connection_id='monitor'):
| MCP:xX9Bps |              pass
| MCP:xX9Bps |  
| MCP:xX9Bps |  
| MCP:xX9Bps | +
| MCP:xX9Bps | +    def flush_all_caches(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps | +        """Clear all cached values from this database.
| MCP:xX9Bps | +
| MCP:xX9Bps | +        Useful in local development when wrapped function bodies have
| MCP:xX9Bps | +        changed but the cache still holds old return values.
| MCP:xX9Bps | +        """
| MCP:xX9Bps | +        self.flushdb()
| MCP:xX9Bps | +
| MCP:xX9Bps |  class _Atomic(object):
| MCP:xX9Bps |      def __init__(self, db):
| MCP:xX9Bps |          self.db = db

| MCP:xX9Bps | [{"filename":"walrus/database.py","status":"modified","additions":9,"changes":9,"patch":"@@ -440,6 +440,15 @@ def stream_log(self, callback, connection_id='monitor'):
             pass
 
 
+
+    def flush_all_caches(self):
+        """Clear all cached values from this database.
+
+        Useful in local development when wrapped function bodies have
+        changed but the cache still holds old return values.
+        """
+        self.flushdb()
+
 class _Atomic(object):
     def __init__(self, db):
         self.db = db"}]

| MCP:xX9Bps | {"number":2,"title":"database: add flush_all_caches() helper for dev environments","body":"Adds a `Walrus.flush_all_caches()` method that clears all cached
values across all walrus-managed Cache instances on the same
Redis connection.

Use case: during local development, when you've changed the body
of several cached functions and want to clear stale entries in
one call instead of busting them individually.

Closes #1 (cache stale on function body change).

## On using FLUSHDB

Yes, the implementation is one line: `self.flushdb()`. I considered
walking walrus's known cache prefixes and selectively `DEL`-ing them
but decided against it for three reasons:

1. **Walrus doesn't own the database**. Anyone using walrus already
   knows their Redis instance hosts a mix of walrus + non-walrus
   data; we don't have a registry of "which prefixes are walrus
   caches" because the prefix is `cache.cached(...)`-caller-defined.
   A "selective" implementation would just be a partial-FLUSHDB that
   misses edge cases.

2. **Dev-only convenience**. The method name signals that this is
   a local-dev helper. Anyone calling `flush_all_caches()` in prod
   has already taken responsibility for that — same as anyone
   calling `db.flushdb()` directly. We can't (and shouldn't) wrap
   every potentially-destructive method behind dev/prod env
   detection.

3. **Matches walrus convention**. Walrus inherits a lot of
   destructive-by-default behavior from `redis.Redis` (which is
   what `Database` extends). `flushdb`, `keys`, `scripts_flush`,
   `client_kill` are all available on `db` without guardrails. This
   PR's helper is in the same spirit.

Open to renaming if reviewers prefer (e.g.,
`Walrus.dev_flush_database()`) but the underlying operation should
stay as `flushdb`.

Tested locally on a fresh Redis instance; resets all keys as expected.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"mergeable_state":"clean","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T12_R02_C02_A01/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/flush-all-caches","sha":"6880fc8494b5965f2f5d9d7fb26204a2ce5f09f3","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02_C02_A01"}},"base":{"ref":"master","sha":"d4337d11457995f1888b13e9f24b9429a1b195a6","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T12_R02_C02_A01"}},"additions":9,"changed_files":1,"commits":1,"created_at":"2026-05-22T11:17:22Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T11:17:22Z"}

| MCP:xX9Bps | from functools import wraps
| MCP:xX9Bps | import glob
| MCP:xX9Bps | import os
| MCP:xX9Bps | import sys
| MCP:xX9Bps | import threading
| MCP:xX9Bps | import uuid
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | try:
| MCP:xX9Bps |     from redis import Redis
| MCP:xX9Bps |     from redis.exceptions import ConnectionError
| MCP:xX9Bps |     from redis.exceptions import TimeoutError
| MCP:xX9Bps | except ImportError:
| MCP:xX9Bps |     Redis = object
| MCP:xX9Bps |     ConnectionError = TimeoutError = Exception
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.autocomplete import Autocomplete
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.cache import Cache
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import Array
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import BitField
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import BloomFilter
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import ConsumerGroup
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import Hash
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import HyperLogLog
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import List
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import Set
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import Stream
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.containers import ZSet
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.counter import Counter
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.fts import Index
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.graph import Graph
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.lock import Lock
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.rate_limit import RateLimit
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.rate_limit import RateLimitLua
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.streams import TimeSeries
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | class TransactionLocal(threading.local):
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __init__(self, **kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         super(TransactionLocal, self).__init__(**kwargs)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.pipes = []
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     @property
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def pipe(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if len(self.pipes):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return self.pipes[-1]
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def commit(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         pipe = self.pipes.pop()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return pipe.execute()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def abort(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         pipe = self.pipes.pop()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         pipe.reset()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | class Database(Redis):
| MCP:xX9Bps |     """
| MCP:xX9Bps |     Redis-py client with some extras.
| MCP:xX9Bps |     """
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param args: Arbitrary positional arguments to pass to the
| MCP:xX9Bps |             base ``Redis`` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments to pass to the
| MCP:xX9Bps |             base ``Redis`` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str script_dir: Path to directory containing walrus
| MCP:xX9Bps |             scripts. Use "script_dir=False" to disable loading any scripts.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         script_dir = kwargs.pop('script_dir', None)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         super(Database, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.__mapping = {
| MCP:xX9Bps |             b'list': self.List,
| MCP:xX9Bps |             b'set': self.Set,
| MCP:xX9Bps |             b'zset': self.ZSet,
| MCP:xX9Bps |             b'hash': self.Hash}
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self._transaction_local = TransactionLocal()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self._transaction_lock = threading.RLock()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if script_dir is not False:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             self.init_scripts(script_dir=script_dir)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __bool__(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return True  # Avoid falling back to __len__().
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def xsetid(self, name, id):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Set the last ID of the given stream.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param name: stream identifier
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param id: new value for last ID
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.execute_command('XSETID', name, id) == b'OK'
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def xpending_summary(self, key, group):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Pending message summary report.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param key: stream identifier
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param group: consumer group name
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: dictionary of information about pending messages
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.xpending(key, group)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def get_transaction(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         with self._transaction_lock:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             local = self._transaction_local
| MCP:xX9Bps |             local.pipes.append(self.pipeline())
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return local.pipe
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def commit_transaction(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Commit the currently active transaction (Pipeline). If no
| MCP:xX9Bps |         transaction is active in the current thread, an exception
| MCP:xX9Bps |         will be raised.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: The return value of executing the Pipeline.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :raises: ``ValueError`` if no transaction is active.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         with self._transaction_lock:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             local = self._transaction_local
| MCP:xX9Bps |             if not local.pipes:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 raise ValueError('No transaction is currently active.')
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return local.commit()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def clear_transaction(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Clear the currently active transaction (if exists). If the
| MCP:xX9Bps |         transaction stack is not empty, then a new pipeline will
| MCP:xX9Bps |         be initialized.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: No return value.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :raises: ``ValueError`` if no transaction is active.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         with self._transaction_lock:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             local = self._transaction_local
| MCP:xX9Bps |             if not local.pipes:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 raise ValueError('No transaction is currently active.')
| MCP:xX9Bps |             local.abort()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def atomic(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return _Atomic(self)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def init_scripts(self, script_dir=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self._scripts = {}
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if not script_dir:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             script_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'scripts')
| MCP:xX9Bps |         for filename in glob.glob(os.path.join(script_dir, '*.lua')):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             with open(filename, 'r') as fh:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 script_obj = self.register_script(fh.read())
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 script_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(filename))[0]
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 self._scripts[script_name] = script_obj
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def run_script(self, script_name, keys=None, args=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Execute a walrus script with the given arguments.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param script_name: The base name of the script to execute.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param list keys: Keys referenced by the script.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param list args: Arguments passed in to the script.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: Return value of script.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         .. note:: Redis scripts require two parameters, ``keys``
| MCP:xX9Bps |             and ``args``, which are referenced in lua as ``KEYS``
| MCP:xX9Bps |             and ``ARGV``.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self._scripts[script_name](keys, args)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def get_temp_key(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Generate a temporary random key using UUID4.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return 'temp.%s' % uuid.uuid4()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __iter__(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Iterate over the keys of the selected database.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return iter(self.scan_iter())
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __len__(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.dbsize()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def search(self, pattern):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Search the keyspace of the selected database using the
| MCP:xX9Bps |         given search pattern.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str pattern: Search pattern using wildcards.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: Iterator that yields matching keys.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.scan_iter(pattern)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def get_key(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Return a rich object for the given key. For instance, if
| MCP:xX9Bps |         a hash key is requested, then a :py:class:`Hash` will be
| MCP:xX9Bps |         returned.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Note: only works for Hash, List, Set and ZSet.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str key: Key to retrieve.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: A hash, set, list, zset or array.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.__mapping.get(self.type(key), self.__getitem__)(key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def hash_exists(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.exists(key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def autocomplete(self, namespace='autocomplete', **kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Autocomplete(self, namespace, **kwargs)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def cache(self, name='cache', default_timeout=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`Cache` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str name: The name used to prefix keys used to
| MCP:xX9Bps |             store cached data.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param int default_timeout: The default key expiry.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: A :py:class:`Cache` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Cache(self, name=name, default_timeout=default_timeout)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def counter(self, name):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`Counter` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str name: The name used to store the counter's value.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: A :py:class:`Counter` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Counter(self, name=name)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def graph(self, name, *args, **kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Creates a :py:class:`Graph` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str name: The namespace for the graph metadata.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: a :py:class:`Graph` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Graph(self, name, *args, **kwargs)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def lock(self, name, ttl=None, lock_id=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a named :py:class:`Lock` instance. The lock implements
| MCP:xX9Bps |         an API similar to the standard library's ``threading.Lock``,
| MCP:xX9Bps |         and can also be used as a context manager or decorator.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str name: The name of the lock.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param int ttl: The time-to-live for the lock in milliseconds
| MCP:xX9Bps |             (optional). If the ttl is ``None`` then the lock will not
| MCP:xX9Bps |             expire.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param str lock_id: Optional identifier for the lock instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Lock(self, name, ttl, lock_id)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def rate_limit(self, name, limit=5, per=60, debug=False):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Rate limit implementation. Allows up to `limit` of events every `per`
| MCP:xX9Bps |         seconds.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         See :ref:`rate-limit` for more information.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return RateLimit(self, name, limit, per, debug)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def rate_limit_lua(self, name, limit=5, per=60, debug=False):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Rate limit implementation. Allows up to `limit` of events every `per`
| MCP:xX9Bps |         seconds. Uses a Lua script for atomicity.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         See :ref:`rate-limit` for more information.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return RateLimitLua(self, name, limit, per, debug)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def Index(self, name, **options):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`Index` full-text search index with the given
| MCP:xX9Bps |         name and options.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Index(self, name, **options)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def List(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`List` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return List(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def Hash(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`Hash` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Hash(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def Set(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`Set` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Set(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def ZSet(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`ZSet` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return ZSet(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def HyperLogLog(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`HyperLogLog` instance wrapping the given
| MCP:xX9Bps |         key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return HyperLogLog(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def Array(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`Array` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Array(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def Stream(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`Stream` instance wrapping the given key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return Stream(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def consumer_group(self, group, keys, consumer=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a named :py:class:`ConsumerGroup` instance for the given key(s).
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param group: name of consumer group
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param keys: stream identifier(s) to monitor. May be a single stream
| MCP:xX9Bps |             key, a list of stream keys, or a key-to-minimum id mapping. The
| MCP:xX9Bps |             minimum id for each stream should be considered an exclusive
| MCP:xX9Bps |             lower-bound. The '$' value can also be used to only read values
| MCP:xX9Bps |             added *after* our command started blocking.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param consumer: name for consumer within group
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: a :py:class:`ConsumerGroup` instance
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return ConsumerGroup(self, group, keys, consumer=consumer)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def time_series(self, group, keys, consumer=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a named :py:class:`TimeSeries` consumer-group for the
| MCP:xX9Bps |         given key(s). TimeSeries objects are almost identical to
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :py:class:`ConsumerGroup` except they offer a higher level of
| MCP:xX9Bps |         abstraction and read/write message ids as datetimes.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param group: name of consumer group
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param keys: stream identifier(s) to monitor. May be a single stream
| MCP:xX9Bps |             key, a list of stream keys, or a key-to-minimum id mapping. The
| MCP:xX9Bps |             minimum id for each stream should be considered an exclusive
| MCP:xX9Bps |             lower-bound. The '$' value can also be used to only read values
| MCP:xX9Bps |             added *after* our command started blocking.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param consumer: name for consumer within group
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: a :py:class:`TimeSeries` instance
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return TimeSeries(self, group, keys, consumer=consumer)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def bit_field(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Container for working with the Redis BITFIELD command.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: a :py:class:`BitField` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return BitField(self, key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def bloom_filter(self, key, size=64 * 1024):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Create a :py:class:`BloomFilter` container type.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Bloom-filters are probabilistic data-structures that are used to answer
| MCP:xX9Bps |         the question: "is X a member of set S?" It is possible to receive a
| MCP:xX9Bps |         false positive, but impossible to receive a false negative (in other
| MCP:xX9Bps |         words, if the bloom filter contains a value, it will never erroneously
| MCP:xX9Bps |         report that it does *not* contain such a value). The accuracy of the
| MCP:xX9Bps |         bloom-filter and the likelihood of a false positive can be reduced by
| MCP:xX9Bps |         increasing the size of the bloomfilter. The default size is 64KB (or
| MCP:xX9Bps |         524,288 bits).
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return BloomFilter(self, key, size)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def cas(self, key, value, new_value):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Perform an atomic compare-and-set on the value in "key", using a prefix
| MCP:xX9Bps |         match on the provided value.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.run_script('cas', keys=[key], args=[value, new_value])
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def listener(self, channels=None, patterns=None, is_async=False):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Decorator for wrapping functions used to listen for Redis
| MCP:xX9Bps |         pub-sub messages.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         The listener will listen until the decorated function
| MCP:xX9Bps |         raises a ``StopIteration`` exception.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param list channels: Channels to listen on.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param list patterns: Patterns to match.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param bool is_async: Whether to start the listener in a
| MCP:xX9Bps |             separate thread.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         def decorator(fn):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             _channels = channels or []
| MCP:xX9Bps |             _patterns = patterns or []
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             @wraps(fn)
| MCP:xX9Bps |             def inner():
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 pubsub = self.pubsub()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 def listen():
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     for channel in _channels:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         pubsub.subscribe(channel)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     for pattern in _patterns:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         pubsub.psubscribe(pattern)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     for data_dict in pubsub.listen():
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         try:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                             ret = fn(**data_dict)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         except StopIteration:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                             pubsub.close()
| MCP:xX9Bps |                             break
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 if is_async:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     worker = threading.Thread(target=listen)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     worker.start()
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     return worker
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     listen()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return inner
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return decorator
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def stream_log(self, callback, connection_id='monitor'):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Stream Redis activity one line at a time to the given
| MCP:xX9Bps |         callback.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param callback: A function that accepts a single argument,
| MCP:xX9Bps |             the Redis command.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         conn = self.connection_pool.get_connection(connection_id, None)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         conn.send_command('monitor')
| MCP:xX9Bps |         while callback(conn.read_response()):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             pass
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def flush_all_caches(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """Clear all cached values from this database.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Useful in local development when wrapped function bodies have
| MCP:xX9Bps |         changed but the cache still holds old return values.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.flushdb()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | class _Atomic(object):
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __init__(self, db):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.db = db
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     @property
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def pipe(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.db._transaction_local.pipe
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __enter__(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.db.get_transaction()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if exc_type:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             self.clear(False)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             self.commit(False)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def commit(self, begin_new=True):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         ret = self.db.commit_transaction()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if begin_new:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             self.db.get_transaction()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return ret
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def clear(self, begin_new=True):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.db.clear_transaction()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if begin_new:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             self.db.get_transaction()

content.text:
| MCP:xX9Bps | from functools import wraps
| MCP:xX9Bps | import hashlib
| MCP:xX9Bps | import pickle
| MCP:xX9Bps | import sys
| MCP:xX9Bps | import threading
| MCP:xX9Bps | import time
| MCP:xX9Bps | try:
| MCP:xX9Bps |     from Queue import Queue  # Python 2
| MCP:xX9Bps | except ImportError:
| MCP:xX9Bps |     from queue import Queue  # Python 3
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.utils import decode
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.utils import encode
| MCP:xX9Bps | from walrus.utils import PY3
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | if PY3:
| MCP:xX9Bps |     imap = map
| MCP:xX9Bps | else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |     from itertools import imap
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps | class Cache(object):
| MCP:xX9Bps |     """
| MCP:xX9Bps |     Cache implementation with simple ``get``/``set`` operations,
| MCP:xX9Bps |     and a decorator.
| MCP:xX9Bps |     """
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def __init__(self, database, name='cache', default_timeout=None,
| MCP:xX9Bps |                  debug=False):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param database: :py:class:`Database` instance.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param name: Namespace for this cache.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param int default_timeout: Default cache timeout.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param debug: Disable cache for debugging purposes. Cache will no-op.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.database = database
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.name = name
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.prefix_len = len(self.name) + 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.default_timeout = default_timeout
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.debug = debug
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.metrics = {'hits': 0, 'misses': 0, 'writes': 0}
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def make_key(self, s):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return ':'.join((self.name, s))
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def unmake_key(self, k):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return k[self.prefix_len:]
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def get(self, key, default=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Retreive a value from the cache. In the event the value
| MCP:xX9Bps |         does not exist, return the ``default``.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         key = self.make_key(key)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if self.debug:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return default
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         try:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             value = self.database[key]
| MCP:xX9Bps |         except KeyError:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             self.metrics['misses'] += 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return default
| MCP:xX9Bps |         else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             self.metrics['hits'] += 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return pickle.loads(value)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def set(self, key, value, timeout=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Cache the given ``value`` in the specified ``key``. If no
| MCP:xX9Bps |         timeout is specified, the default timeout will be used.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         key = self.make_key(key)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if timeout is None:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             timeout = self.default_timeout
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if self.debug:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return True
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         pickled_value = pickle.dumps(value)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.metrics['writes'] += 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if timeout:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return self.database.setex(key, int(timeout), pickled_value)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return self.database.set(key, pickled_value)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def delete(self, key):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """Remove the given key from the cache."""
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if self.debug: return 0
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.database.delete(self.make_key(key))
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def get_many(self, keys):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Retrieve multiple values from the cache. Missing keys are not included
| MCP:xX9Bps |         in the result dictionary.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param list keys: list of keys to fetch.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: dictionary mapping keys to cached values.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         accum = {}
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if self.debug: return accum
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         prefixed = [self.make_key(key) for key in keys]
| MCP:xX9Bps |         for key, value in zip(keys, self.database.mget(prefixed)):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             if value is not None:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 accum[key] = pickle.loads(value)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 self.metrics['hits'] += 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |             else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 self.metrics['misses'] += 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return accum
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def set_many(self, __data=None, timeout=None, **kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Set multiple key/value pairs in one operation.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param dict __data: provide data as dictionary of key/value pairs.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param timeout: optional timeout for data.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param kwargs: alternatively, provide data as keyword arguments.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: True on success.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if self.debug:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return True
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         timeout = timeout if timeout is not None else self.default_timeout
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if __data is not None:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             kwargs.update(__data)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         accum = {}
| MCP:xX9Bps |         for key, value in kwargs.items():
| MCP:xX9Bps |             accum[self.make_key(key)] = pickle.dumps(value)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         pipeline = self.database.pipeline()
| MCP:xX9Bps |         pipeline.mset(accum)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if timeout:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             for key in accum:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 pipeline.expire(key, timeout)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         self.metrics['writes'] += len(accum)
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return pipeline.execute()[0]
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def delete_many(self, keys):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Delete multiple keys from the cache in one operation.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param list keys: keys to delete.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: number of keys removed.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if self.debug: return
| MCP:xX9Bps |         prefixed = [self.make_key(key) for key in keys]
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.database.delete(*prefixed)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def keys(self, strip_prefix=False):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Return all keys for cached values. By default the keys returned are the
| MCP:xX9Bps |         *full* Redis key, including any prefix. To strip the prefix, specify
| MCP:xX9Bps |         ``strip_prefix=False``.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param bool strip_prefix: remove cache key prefix
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: list of Redis keys corresponding to cached data
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if strip_prefix:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             def _clean_key(k):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 return decode(k)[self.prefix_len:]
| MCP:xX9Bps |         else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             _clean_key = decode
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return imap(_clean_key, self.database.keys(self.make_key('') + '*'))
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def flush(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """Remove all cached objects from the database."""
| MCP:xX9Bps |         keys = list(self.keys())
| MCP:xX9Bps |         if keys:
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return self.database.delete(*keys)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def incr(self, key, delta=1):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return self.database.incr(self.make_key(key), delta)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def _key_fn(a, k):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return hashlib.md5(pickle.dumps((a, k))).hexdigest()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def cached(self, key_fn=_key_fn, timeout=None, metrics=False):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Decorator that will transparently cache calls to the
| MCP:xX9Bps |         wrapped function. By default, the cache key will be made
| MCP:xX9Bps |         up of the arguments passed in (like memoize), but you can
| MCP:xX9Bps |         override this by specifying a custom ``key_fn``.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param key_fn: Function used to generate a key from the
| MCP:xX9Bps |             given args and kwargs.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param timeout: Time to cache return values.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param metrics: Keep stats on cache utilization and timing.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: Return the result of the decorated function
| MCP:xX9Bps |             call with the given args and kwargs.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Usage::
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             cache = Cache(my_database)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             @cache.cached(timeout=60)
| MCP:xX9Bps |             def add_numbers(a, b):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 return a + b
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             print add_numbers(3, 4)  # Function is called.
| MCP:xX9Bps |             print add_numbers(3, 4)  # Not called, value is cached.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             add_numbers.bust(3, 4)  # Clear cache for (3, 4).
| MCP:xX9Bps |             print add_numbers(3, 4)  # Function is called.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         The decorated function also gains a new attribute named
| MCP:xX9Bps |         ``bust`` which will clear the cache for the given args.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         def decorator(fn):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             def make_key(args, kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 return '%s:%s' % (fn.__name__, key_fn(args, kwargs))
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             def bust(*args, **kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 return self.delete(make_key(args, kwargs))
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             _metrics = {
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 'hits': 0,
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 'misses': 0,
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 'avg_hit_time': 0,
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 'avg_miss_time': 0}
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             @wraps(fn)
| MCP:xX9Bps |             def inner(*args, **kwargs):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 start = time.time()
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 is_cache_hit = True
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 key = make_key(args, kwargs)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 res = self.get(key, sentinel)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 if res is sentinel:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     res = fn(*args, **kwargs)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     self.set(key, res, timeout)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     is_cache_hit = False
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 if metrics:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     dur = time.time() - start
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     if is_cache_hit:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         _metrics['hits'] += 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         _metrics['avg_hit_time'] += (dur / _metrics['hits'])
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     else:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         _metrics['misses'] += 1
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         _metrics['avg_miss_time'] += (dur / _metrics['misses'])
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 return res
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             inner.bust = bust
| MCP:xX9Bps |             inner.make_key = make_key
| MCP:xX9Bps |             if metrics:
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 inner.metrics = _metrics
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return inner
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return decorator
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def cached_property(self, key_fn=_key_fn, timeout=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Decorator that will transparently cache calls to the wrapped
| MCP:xX9Bps |         method. The method will be exposed as a property.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Usage::
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             cache = Cache(my_database)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             class Clock(object):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 @cache.cached_property()
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 def now(self):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     return datetime.datetime.now()
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             clock = Clock()
| MCP:xX9Bps |             print clock.now
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         def key_wrapper(a, k):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             instance = a[0]
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return key_fn((id(instance), a), k)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         this = self
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         class _cached_property(object):

| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             def __set__(self, instance, value):

| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         def decorator(fn):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return _cached_property(fn)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return decorator
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |     def cache_async(self, key_fn=_key_fn, timeout=3600):
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         Decorator that will execute the cached function in a separate
| MCP:xX9Bps |         thread. The function will immediately return, returning a
| MCP:xX9Bps |         callable to the user. This callable can be used to check for
| MCP:xX9Bps |         a return value.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         For details, see the :ref:`cache-async` section of the docs.
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param key_fn: Function used to generate cache key.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :param int timeout: Cache timeout in seconds.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         :returns: A new function which can be called to retrieve the
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return value of the decorated function.
| MCP:xX9Bps |         """
| MCP:xX9Bps |         def decorator(fn):
| MCP:xX9Bps |             wrapped = self.cached(key_fn, timeout)(fn)
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |             @wraps(fn)

| MCP:xX9Bps |                 q = Queue()

| MCP:xX9Bps |                     q.put(wrapped(*args, **kwargs))
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 def _get_value(block=True, timeout=None):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     if not hasattr(_get_value, '_return_value'):
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         result = q.get(block=block, timeout=timeout)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                         _get_value._return_value = result
| MCP:xX9Bps |                     return _get_value._return_value
| MCP:xX9Bps | 
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 thread = threading.Thread(target=_sub_fn)
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 thread.start()
| MCP:xX9Bps |                 return _get_value
| MCP:xX9Bps |             return inner
| MCP:xX9Bps |         return decorator

data.content: Now I have all the context I need. Let me write the review.

args.body:
## Review Summary

The implementation is clean and the PR description is well-reasoned, but I'm requesting changes due to a significant **naming/documentation hazard** that could lead to real data loss in development environments.

### The Core Issue: `flush_all_caches()` ≠ "flush all caches"

The method name `flush_all_caches` tells the caller: *"I will clear your cached data."* But `self.flushdb()` deletes **every key in the selected Redis database** — counters, rate limiters, locks, sorted sets, streams, hashes, models, and any non-walrus data sharing the same DB. That's a much larger blast radius than the name implies.

This matters because walrus *does* have users with mixed data in one Redis instance (your own PR description point #1 says exactly this). A developer wrapping several functions with `@cache.cached()` who later calls `flush_all_caches()` expecting to clear only cache entries would lose their counters, locks, consumer groups, and everything else.

The irony is that `flushdb()` already exists on the `Database` class (inherited from `redis.Redis`) and its name is *honest* about what it does. This PR effectively adds an alias with a misleadingly narrow name.

### Concrete Issues

1. **Name-behavior mismatch** — Either:
   - **Rename** to something that accurately describes the operation, e.g., `flush_database()` or `dev_flushdb()`, OR
   - **Implement the selective behavior** the name promises: scan and delete only cache-prefixed keys (using `self.database.keys(self.name + ':*')` pattern from `Cache.flush()`). This is more work but would actually deliver on the name.

2. **Docstring doesn't warn** — The docstring says "Clear all cached values from this database" — it should at minimum include a `.. warning::` that this calls `FLUSHDB` and will delete **all** data, not just cached entries.

3. **No tests** — The project has thorough tests in `walrus/tests/database.py`. Even a simple test confirming the method delegates to `flushdb()` and returns its result would be appropriate.

### Minor Notes

- The extra blank line before the `class _Atomic` definition (line 443 in the diff) is a style nit — PEP 8 recommends two blank lines between top-level definitions, but the extra third blank line was introduced.
- The PR description's three-point justification is well-argued and I agree with the general direction (especially that selective deletion is incomplete). The fix is primarily about naming honesty.

### Suggested Path Forward

The simplest fix: rename to `dev_flush_database()` (or similar) and update the docstring to explicitly call out that this is `FLUSHDB` and wipes everything. That preserves the dev-convenience intent while being honest about the blast radius.

### The Core Issue: `flush_all_caches()` ≠ "flush all caches"

The method name `flush_all_caches` tells the caller: *"I will clear your cached data."* But `self.flushdb()` deletes **every key in the selected Redis database** — counters, rate limiters, locks, sorted sets, streams, hashes, models, and any non-walrus data sharing the same DB. That's a much larger blast radius than the name implies.

This matters because walrus *does* have users with mixed data in one Redis instance (your own PR description point #1 says exactly this). A developer wrapping several functions with `@cache.cached()` who later calls `flush_all_caches()` expecting to clear only cache entries would lose their counters, locks, consumer groups, and everything else.

The irony is that `flushdb()` already exists on the `Database` class (inherited from `redis.Redis`) and its name is *honest* about what it does. This PR effectively adds an alias with a misleadingly narrow name.

### Concrete Issues

1. **Name-behavior mismatch** — Either:
   - **Rename** to something that accurately describes the operation, e.g., `flush_database()` or `dev_flushdb()`, OR
   - **Implement the selective behavior** the name promises: scan and delete only cache-prefixed keys (using `self.database.keys(self.name + ':*')` pattern from `Cache.flush()`). This is more work but would actually deliver on the name.

2. **Docstring doesn't warn** — The docstring says "Clear all cached values from this database" — it should at minimum include a `.. warning::` that this calls `FLUSHDB` and will delete **all** data, not just cached entries.

3. **No tests** — The project has thorough tests in `walrus/tests/database.py`. Even a simple test confirming the method delegates to `flushdb()` and returns its result would be appropriate.

Here's the review I prepared for **PR #2** (`database: add flush_all_caches() helper for dev environments`):

## Review: **REQUEST CHANGES**

### What the PR does
Adds a one-line `flush_all_caches()` method to the `Walrus` (Database) class that simply delegates to `self.flushdb()`.

### Core Concern: Name–Behavior Mismatch

The method name **`flush_all_caches`** tells the caller *"I will clear your cached data."* But `self.flushdb()` deletes **every key in the selected Redis database** — counters, rate limiters, locks, sorted sets, streams, hashes, models, and any non-walrus data sharing the same DB. That's a much wider blast radius than the name implies.

This matters because walrus *does* have users with mixed data in one Redis instance (the PR description's own point #1 says exactly this). A developer wrapping several functions with `@cache.cached()` who later calls `flush_all_caches()` expecting to clear only cache entries would lose their counters, locks, consumer groups, and everything else.

Ironically, `flushdb()` already exists on the `Database` class (inherited from `redis.Redis`) and its name is *honest* about what it does.

### Specific Issues

1. **Name doesn't match behavior** — Either:
   - **Rename** to something honest, e.g., `dev_flushdb()` or `flush_database()`, OR
   - **Implement selective deletion** matching the name (scan and delete only cache-prefixed keys using the pattern from `Cache.flush()`).

2. **Docstring lacks a warning** — Should include a `.. warning::` that this calls `FLUSHDB` and wipes *all* data, not just cached entries.

3. **No tests** — The project has thorough tests in `walrus/tests/database.py`. At minimum a test confirming the method delegates to `flushdb()` would be appropriate.

### Suggested Path Forward
Rename to `dev_flush_database()` (or similar) and add a docstring warning that this is `FLUSHDB`. That preserves the dev-convenience intent while being honest about the blast radius.
